evidence_assessments.supported_claim: Confirms a fault output exists (o.c. 40 mA) and explains the logic: outputs are Normally Energised open collectors associated to alarm thresholds and fault status, i.e., they de-energise on failure.
Shows mounting rules and terminal strip layout but does not establish the fault output or its failure logic.
Identification code shows open-collector mode options P=positive safety (energized) and N=negative safety, contextualising the output logic variants.
Cover page and index only; no technical content on fault output or logic.
Terminal 8 is the O.C. Fault output; states all SMART 2 outputs are open collector in positive safety (sicurezza positiva) with negative switching, max 40 mA.
States the detector provides an Open Collector output associated with fault, plus self-diagnostic algorithm that constantly checks hardware including the sensor.
Testing and use instructions; no fault output information.
Describes LED/output behaviour for negative safety (JN) TWIN versions at power-on, adding context about output logic variants.
Maintenance, dismounting routines; unrelated to fault output logic.
Cable sizing and connection guidance; does not address fault output or failure logic.
Technical specs: self-diagnostic routine with O.C. 40 mA fault output; output logic is Sicurezza Positiva / fail safe (standard) or fail in alarm.
Index page only; no answer content.
Italian wiring/connection guidance; no fault output logic details.
Jumper configuration for alarm thresholds; unrelated to fault output.
English description: three O.C. threshold outputs plus a fourth O.C. output for Fault, with self-diagnostic procedure monitoring hardware and sensor.

judgements.rationale: Yes: SMART 2 has an O.C. 40 mA fault output tied to the self-diagnostic routine (E-A1334F9996F8, E-485E42D88FC6, E-FD524C1DE741). The logic is positive safety / fail-safe: outputs are normally energised open collectors that de-energise on fault (E-026ED7172265, E-3615653EA383, E-A1334F9996F8), with an optional negative safety variant (E-1DAD917B69F8). E-A1334F9996F8 plus E-026ED7172265 form the smallest sufficient set covering both existence and failure logic.
